title: Load‚Äč
content: </> Cardinal.Load( Parent::¬†Instance , LoadOptions:¬†{ Deep:¬†boolean?, Predicate:¬†((ModuleScript )¬†‚Üí¬†boolean)?, Exclusions:¬†{string}? }? ) ‚Üí¬†Promise<string> Loads a ModuleScript into the Loader. LoadOptions: Deep is an optional boolean that will determine if the Loader should load all ModuleScripts in the Parent's descendants. Predicate is an optional function that will be used to determine if the ModuleScript should be loaded. Exclusions is an optional table of strings that will be used to exclude certain ModuleScripts from being loaded.

title: Start‚Äč
content: </> Cardinal.Start(Options::¬†{ ServicePromise:¬†boolean?, Timeout:¬†number? }?) ‚Üí¬†Promise<string> Starts the Loader, initializing and starting all services/controllers. Options: ServicePromise is an optional boolean that will determine if the client callback functions should return a Promise. Timeout is an optional number that will determine the timeout for the initialization and starting of the services/controllers.

title: GetService‚Äč
content: This item only works when running on the client. Client </> Cardinal.GetService(Name::¬†string) ‚Üí¬†table¬†|¬†nil Gets a service from the Loader by name (client only, and case sensitive).

title: OnStart‚Äč
content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> Cardinal.OnStart() ‚Üí¬†Promise<nil> Waits for the Loader to start.

title: AddCustomLifecycle‚Äč
content: </> Cardinal.AddCustomLifecycle( Name::¬†string, Signal::¬†any ) ‚Üí¬†() Adds a custom lifecycle event to the Loader. This will allow you to add custom events that can be used in your services/controllers.
